briefdescription.para: This function runs a test on the NAND flash device using the basic driver functions.

detaileddescription.para:
The function does the following tasks:
Initialize the driver.
Erase the required length of bytes by taking bad blocks into account.
Write the number of bytes from given offset by taking bad blocks into account.
Read the number of bytes from given offset by taking bad blocks into account.
Compare the data read against the data Written.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the data from the Flash device and copies it into the specified user buffer.

detaileddescription.para: This function considers bad blocks and skips them to read next blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the flash data address to read from.
is number of bytes to read.
is the destination address to copy data to.

briefdescription.para: This function programs the flash device(s) with data specified in the user buffer.

detaileddescription.para: This function considers bad blocks and skips them.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the flash data address to write to.
is number of bytes to write.
is the source address to write the data from.

briefdescription.para: This function erases length bytes in the flash device from a given offset.

detaileddescription.para: The Offset and Length must be block aligned. This functions skips bad blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNand instance.
is the flash address to start erasing from.
is number of bytes to erase.

briefdescription.para: Main function to execute the Skip Block based Nand read/write example.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the example has completed successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the example has failed.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the length including bad blocks from a given offset and length.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the flash data address to read from.
is number of bytes to read.

listitem.para: Return actual length including bad blocks.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a design example using the NAND driver (XNandPs).

detaileddescription.para: This example tests the skip block method of erase/read/write operations. The skip block method is useful while reading/writing images on to the flash. The flash is erased and programming by considering the bad blocks. The data is read back and compared with the data written for correctness.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 nm 12/10/2010 First release 1.01a nm 28/02/2012 Modified the test offsets. ms 04/10/17 Modified Comment lines to follow doxygen rules 2.8 akm 07/06/23 Add support for system device-tree flow. 2.11 vlt 12/14/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
